data.title: "A Bad Contract for the Last Decade." Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Plans Huge New Deliveries as Finished Goods Cushion Dwindles
data.description:
Finished goods inventory can be a thorny issue. A certain amount of it is vital. A little more than that is considered prudent. But the line between prudence and waste can be thin, and will vary from person to person. Aerospace giant Boeing (BA) discovered the value of inventory in the last year, but now, Boeing plans big new deliveries and very little cushion to support them. This is not sitting well with investors, who sent Boeing shares down fractionally in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Boeing wants to deliver 500 737 aircraft this year, in various sizes, as it looks to step up its production through 2026 and eventually get another bump to the production cap established by the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA). But Boeing also warned that its ability to meet that goal will be a little tougher this year thanks to the loss of its production cushion.

Basically, Boeing noted, it will be handing over the last 737 made before 2023 sometime in the next two months. That means the planes it hands over now will be the planes it produces in the moment. Given that Boeing, at last report, has a production cap of 42 737 planes per month, this means Boeing will need about 11.9 months to actually make 500 planes. How fortunate that there are 12 months in the year.

"A Bad Contract for the Last Decade"

Meanwhile, Boeing also reported a loss of $565 million on the KC-46 refueling tanker. This plane has brought in billions of dollars in losses over the last several years, and Boeing reportedly looks forward to repricing the tanker.

Boeing's Kelly Ortberg declared that the loss was "disappointing," but noted that the aircraft itself was performing well. This should help ensure that the KC-46 is a big part of Boeing's sales going forward. And when the aircraft can be repriced, that should change things. Ortberg noted, "This has been a bad contract for the last decade. As we enter into a new opportunity where we get to reprice, we want to make sure that we … ensure it's a fair contract and we can make money."

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 14 Buys, two Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 40.83% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $266.64 per share implies 9.14% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Fed Holds Rates Between 3.50% and 3.75% as Two Officials Dissent
data.description:

data.title: Google Adds More AI Features to Its Chrome Browser
data.description:
Google parent company Alphabet (GOOGL) is adding more artificial intelligence (AI) features to its popular Chrome internet search browser.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The technology giant says new Gemini AI-powered features being added to Chrome include the image generation tool "Nano Banana," Google's "Personal Intelligence" feature, and "Auto browse," a tool that lets users command the browser to perform various tasks across the web with a single prompt.

Alphabet is also introducing a new Chrome design that includes a panel on the right side of the browser that keeps the Gemini chatbot available as users search the internet. These additions are the latest effort by Alphabet to expand the use and adoption of its Gemini AI technology.

AI and Internet Browsers

That's key for the company as it competes against the likes of ChatGPT maker OpenAI and Claude developer Anthropic as browsers are the primary entry point for users to interact with AI services.

Additionally, the new features show how Google is trying to make its Chrome browser more personalized for users. Earlier this month, Google launched the personalized feature "Personal Intelligence" in the Gemini app. The feature connects information from apps like Gmail and Google Photos to provide users personalized answers in the Gemini chatbot.

"Chrome will remember context from past conversations so you get uniquely tailored answers to whatever you're looking for across the web and you can already add specific instructions to Gemini to get more tailored responses," wrote Parisa Tabriz, Chrome vice president, in a blog.

The updates also show the company is trying to realize Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis's vision for building out a "universal assistant," which he has defined as a helper that can plan and take actions on a user's behalf across any device.

Google on Wednesday said AI Pro and Ultra subscribers in the U.S will get access to Auto browse. That agentic features are designed to allow users to ask the Gemini chatbot to perform actions like order groceries or research hotels and flights across multiple date options.

Users can ask Auto browse to "Go through my favorited apartments on Redfin and remove any that are not pet friendly," the company said in its blog.

Google has been ramping up the addition of AI features to its browser since a U.S. district judge ruled in September against forcing the company to sell off Chrome as a consequence of being found to hold an illegal monopoly in the market of internet search. Google earlier this month filed to appeal the ruling that it holds a monopoly in search.

Shortly after the remedies ruling, the company in September updated Chrome to include more AI features that integrate Google apps like Calendar, YouTube and Maps so users can access those services without moving to a different webpage.

OpenAI launched its own browser in October. Atlas, as the browser is called, is built around OpenAI's generative chatbot ChatGPT. The launch of Atlas sent shares of Google parent Alphabet down 2%.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Gold Surges Past $5,300 as Trump Downplays Dollar Weakness
data.description:
Another day, another record high for gold (XAUUSD). On Wednesday, the precious metal surpassed $5,300 per troy ounce for the first time, driven by a weak U.S. dollar spurred by President Trump shrugging off concerns.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I think it's great," Trump said when asked by a reporter if the dollar had fallen too much. "The dollar's doing great." Gold tends to benefit when the dollar weakens, as it becomes cheaper for foreign buyers and more attractive as a store of value.

Dollar Dips, Then Recovers After Bessent Eases Concerns

On Tuesday, the dollar, as measured by the U.S. Dollar Index, fell by 1.3%, marking its worst performance since April 2024. The index tracks the dollar against a basket of six major foreign currencies.

The greenback is staging a partial recovery today after Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent sought to ease fears and emphasized that the U.S. would maintain its strong-dollar policy without involving itself in foreign currency affairs.

data.title: 'Buy Instacart Stock amid Peak Tactical Fear of Amazon,' Says Five-Star Analyst
data.description:
E-commerce giant Amazon (AMZN) has been growing its presence in the grocery delivery market, which has weighed heavily on the stock of rival Instacart (CART). However, five-star Morgan Stanley (MS) analyst Brian Nowak says that this decline may actually be a buying opportunity. Indeed, the grocery market is massive, with an estimated total value of $1.7 trillion, making it one of the largest remaining areas of offline spending. And although Amazon is a serious competitor, Nowak believes that its recent announcements have created what he calls "peak tactical fear" around Instacart.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As a result, that fear may be overdone, especially with Instacart set to report fourth-quarter results on February 12. In fact, the company is expected to deliver adjusted earnings of $0.95 per share on about $970 million in revenue, which would represent strong growth from a year ago. Therefore, Nowak argues that dismissing Instacart because of Amazon's scale would be a mistake. He points to Instacart's loyal subscriber base and notes that customers are often reluctant to switch services once they are used to the convenience.

He also highlights Instacart's strong position in benefiting from AI-related trends, thanks to its access to retailer data, logistics, and its partnership with ChatGPT. Finally, Nowak believes that Amazon poses a bigger threat to smaller competitors than to Instacart itself. He notes that Amazon's early grocery efforts tend to focus on smaller, add-on purchases, while Instacart is more focused on full weekly grocery orders. So while Amazon offers convenience and one-stop shopping across many categories, Instacart stands out for its wider grocery selection and more personalized experience, including real-time communication with shoppers.

Is CART Stock a Good Buy?

Overall, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on CART stock based on 14 Buys, nine Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average CART price target of $50.83 per share implies 34.8% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Slips as France Pulls Back on Videoconferencing Services
data.description:
Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) has its fingers in a wide range of pies. However, French videoconferencing infrastructure is no longer one of these, according to a recent report. France is moving to get Microsoft, and a range of other brands, out of its videoconferencing operations in favor of less reliance on "non-European platforms." This did not sit well with Microsoft shareholders, who sent shares down fractionally in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Basically, the French government threw over its current package—which included Microsoft Teams, GoTo Meeting and several others—in favor of Visio, a secure videoconferencing tool that the Interministerial Digital Directorate put together. The French government intends to put Visio to work in all state operations by 2027, which will cut back on the amount of services it uses that were not made in Europe.

The French government is tired of what some reports call a "patchwork of UC and collaboration tools" dominating the landscape. This patchwork raises costs as well as security risks, and complicates communication between departments. By going to a single standardized service, reports suggest, the French government can both improve security and reduce costs. The fact that the service is home-grown and supports the local economy likely does not hurt either.

Growing Support for Sony

Despite this being Xbox's 25th anniversary, there are growing concerns about its ability to compete in the field. With some signs emerging to suggest that Microsoft may not really be competing any longer, though, this all could change quickly. But for the time being, some are recommending gamers move to Sony's (SONY) PlayStation 5 over the Xbox Series X or S.

The reason is stark, and distressing. It effectively boils down to game access. While both platforms have access to cloud-based gaming platforms offering a wide range of titles, reports suggest that the PS5 will likely have the edge in total number for the rest of this console generation. Admittedly, there is not much time left to this console generation, so your mileage may vary. Still, the suggestion that Sony may have the edge for a while may do some damage to Microsoft's bottom line.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 33 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After an 8.65% rally in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $618.85 per share implies 29.08% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Notches Up With Pilot Partnership
data.description:
While electric vehicle giant Tesla's (TSLA) status as an electric vehicle giant is under fire these days, there are some signs its move to be part of the electric semi race is taking shape. Tesla recently launched a partnership effort with Pilot Travel Centers to bring charging stations to the truck stops. The move proved welcome for investors, who sent shares up fractionally in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla will bring its line of Semi Chargers to several Pilot stations, some of which will be active as early as this summer. Most of the stops will be along I-5, I-10, and "several major corridors where the need for heavy-duty charging is highest." The Pilot stations that have Tesla Semi Chargers will have them in blocks of four to eight, reports note, and use the V4 cabinet charging technology to deliver power faster.

Pilot's senior vice president of alternative fuels, Shannon Sturgil, noted, "Helping to shape the future of energy is a strategic pillar in meeting the needs of our guests and the North American transportation industry. Heavy-duty charging is yet another extension of our exploration into alternative fuel offerings, and we're happy to partner with a leader in the space that provides turnkey solutions and deploys them quickly."

Puzzling Name Change May Be Ahead

Tesla is also planning a move that might puzzle some car buyers, as it sets out to change the name of the Model Y, but only the base model of the Model Y. Thankfully, the name change here should prove helpful to most, as Tesla renames the Model Y Standard to the Model Y Rear-Wheel Drive. Oddly, the other model in the Model Y series, the Model Y Premium, also comes with rear-wheel drive, so the distinction might be a bit puzzling.

Tesla, meanwhile, has not confirmed the name change, so this may never actually happen. Those interested in buying a Tesla will need to check the website, or hit a dealership, to find out if the name change has gone through. Still, why Tesla suddenly feels the need to highlight a feature on the Standard so heavily is, at best, unclear.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 10 Buys, eight Holds, and six Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 10.74% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $406.87 per share implies 6.05% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: 3 Best Dividend Aristocrat Stocks to Buy Now, 01/28/2026
data.description:
If you're aiming to assemble a reliable income portfolio in 2026, dividend aristocrat stocks can be an attractive route to locking in a steady flow of cash distributions. These three stocks are top picks: General Dynamics (GD), Cincinnati Financial (CINF), and A. O. Smith Corporation (AOS).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Which Dividend Aristocrat Stocks Are the Best to Buy?

According to Wall Street analysts, the three stocks listed below are worth buying. Each stock has a dividend payout ratio of at least 45%, meaning that each company distributes at 45% of its net earnings to shareholders as dividends. Furthermore, each company currently has a Buy rating based on consensus from top analysts or broader Wall Street agreement, with significant upside potential.

Be sure to click on any ticker to thoroughly research the stock before you decide whether to add it to your portfolio. Here are today's top dividend aristocrat stock picks from TipRanks.

  1. General Dynamics (GD) — General Dynamics is an American aerospace and defense company. The Virginia-based company's payout ratio is currently 78.77%. General Dynamics paid $1.50 per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 1.65%. GD stock currently holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating from five Wall Street analysts, with an average price target of $398.50, implying 11.48% upside potential.
  2. Cincinnati Financial (CINF) — Cincinnati Financial is an American company that specializes in providing property casualty insurance products. The Ohio-based company's dividend payout ratio currently stands at 45.08%. Cincinnati paid 87 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 2.20%. CINF stock currently holds a Moderate Buy consensus from five analysts on Wall Street, with an average price target of $175 that suggests 10.46% upside.
  3. A. O. Smith Corporation (AOS)  — This is an American water solutions company that manufactures residential and commercial water heaters, boilers, tanks, and water treatment products. The Wisconsin-based company currently has a dividend payout ratio of 72.44%. It paid 36 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 1.94%. AOS stock currently holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating from seven Wall Street analysts, with an average price target of $77.17, implying approximately 10% upside potential.

What Are Dividend Aristocrats?

Dividend aristocrats are a small cadre of companies within the S&P 500 (SPX) that have managed to not only issue a dividend every single year, but also ratchet those payments higher annually for at least a quarter of a century. That kind of long-term dividend discipline tends to appeal to investors who care more about a stable, growing income stream than about trying to time sharp, short-lived moves in share prices.

On top of that, newly created spinoff companies can qualify for dividend aristocrat status as well, since the market generally assumes they inherit their former parent's established habit of raising dividends year after year.

The page provides an exhaustive list of all dividend aristocrats, including their most recent dividend yields, previous dividend amounts, payout ratios, and other details.

data.title: Last-Minute Call: Wall Street Weighs In on Tesla (TSLA) Ahead of Tonight's Earnings
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) heads into its fourth-quarter earnings report tonight, with Wall Street making its final calls before the closing bell. One key piece of the puzzle is already in – fourth-quarter delivery figures – and they didn't exactly light up the scoreboard.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla missed consensus expectations for both Q4 and the full year, delivering 418,227 vehicles, below the Street's forecast of 422,850 in Q4, while for FY25, total deliveries reached 1,636,129 vehicles, below the prognosticators' 1,640,752 target. Sales in the quarter were largely impacted by the expiration of the EV tax credit in September and, ultimately, FY25 represented the company's second straight year of declining auto sales.

With Tesla likely to outline its 2026 vehicle delivery outlook on the earnings call, investors will obviously be keen to find out whether it guides to growth in its auto business or signals another year of lower sales.

Numbers-wise, consensus is calling for 4Q25 revenue of approximately $24.5 billion, GAAP gross margin of around 17.0%, and non-GAAP diluted EPS of $0.43.

Investors will also be watching for updates from Elon Musk on Tesla's robotaxi ambitions. With the safety driver recently removed from testing in Austin, Musk is expected to share more color on expansion plans into multiple metro areas.

On the software side, Tesla continues to push its Full Self-Driving ambitions. After introducing FSD (Supervised) in Australia and New Zealand in Q3, the company is laying the groundwork for a broader rollout in China and a first launch in Europe, pending regulatory approval. Together, these updates could shape how investors view Tesla's growth narrative beyond cars alone.

Cantor analyst Andres Sheppard thinks the forthcoming commercialization of FSD in China and Europe are "material catalysts" with margin upside.

Separately, at the World Economic Forum, Musk indicated that Tesla's humanoid robot, Optimus, is expected to be available for consumer purchase beginning in 2H27. MarketsandMarkets also recently provided its outlook for the humanoid robotics market, estimating global growth from approximately $2.9 billion in 2025 to $15.3 billion by 2030, implying a 39.2% CAGR.

"We believe this is a material opportunity for TSLA over the long term, and on the call, we hope for further details around Optimus' mass production timeline, fleet size, or ASPs," Sheppard noted.

Sheppard is also looking for progress on another autonomous angle – trucking. The analyst continues to believe Tesla will enter the self-driving truck market this decade and expects an update on the timing of Semi production, for which he sees SOP (start of production) this year.

Ahead of the print, Sheppard assigns Tesla shares an Overweight (i.e., Buy) rating, backed by a $510 price target. Should the figure be met, investors will be pocketing returns of 17% a year from now. (To watch Sheppard's track record, click here)

9 other analysts are in the bullish camp, but they're balanced out by 8 Holds and 7 Sells, leaving Tesla with a lukewarm Hold (i.e., Neutral) consensus rating overall. Meanwhile, the Street's average price target of $398.38 suggests the stock could slip about 8% over the next 12 months. (See TSLA stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel
